content.full_text:
June 5, 1976

Mr. Frank E. Barnyak
Director
Freedom and Individual Rights
P. O. Box 65
North Hollywood, California 91603

Dear Mr. Barnyak:

In response to your letter of May 28, 1976 requesting information concerning possible filing requirements under the Political Reform Act, please be assured that with the price of postage those days, you will not receive ten pounds of material at taxpayers' expense.

I will attempt, however, to answer your questions and have enclosed some relevant informational material.

The questions posed in numbers one and two are really part of the same question. If you contribute raises $300 for the purpose of supporting city, county or state candidates or ballot measures, it is a "recipient committee" under the law. If your organization expends $300 in the form of non-monetary (in-kind) contributions of goods or services or repays a loan in the amount of $300 for a candidate or campaign committee, it is an "expenditure" committee under the law. Please refer to pages 3 through 7 of the enclosed "Information Manual" for details of your filing obligations. Also enclosed for your information are regulations 18215 and 18225 which define expenditures and contributions.

Forms you may need to fulfill your filing requirements may be obtained from the appropriate filing officer. (See pages 6 and 7 of the enclosed manual.)

For information concerning your filing obligations as a result of activity in support or opposition of federal candidates or committees, please contact the Federal Election Commission, 1325 K Street, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20005.

In response to your question number three, I have enclosed an informational sheet which outlines your organization's filing obligations if it expend $250 or more in a month to influence legislative or administrative action. Forms may be obtained from the Secretary of State's office, P. O. Box 1467,Sacramento.

Very truly yours,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator
cc: Jeanne-A

FIREARMS AND
INDIVIDUAL RIGHTS

fair
P.O. BOX 65 NORTH HOLLYWOOD, CA. 91603.

May 28, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Calif.

Gentlemen:

1/ Beginning on or about June 15, 1976, we intend to prepare
publish and distribute a list of state, county, city and
Federal candidates/incumbents through retail, wholesale
outlets, shows, events, etc. a "Recommendation for Voting
Consideration Guide" for the November 1976 and future
elections.

2/ We may hold some form of fund raising event for several
of these individuals.

3/ We may print and distribute information related to any
given legislative bill and advise the public of the position
taken by any given legislator. We will inform legislators
our opinion on these bills.

If under the Fair Political Practices Act, we must comply with
some forms, etc., please send them as applicable to 1, 2 and 3
above. Are we considered a lobbyist?

Please do not place 10 lbs. of forms, laws, rules, etc. in a
single brown envelope and just mail to us in reply to this
request.

Please give us a separate group and indicate in red that which
is applicable to our interest under 1, 2 and 3 above.

If you just want to send more for information, please keep it
separate.

Yours very truly,

Frank E. Barnyak
Director

THE UNITED VOICE OF SPORTSMEN - COLLECTORS FOR "FAIR" LEGISLATION
